stages.objective: Observe throughput-relevant execution patterns in shared CPU workload.
stages.goal: Use trace evidence to explain why work completion rate changes.
stages.theory_detail: Throughput measures completed work per time and depends on scheduling overhead and run mix.

hints.nudge: Inspect completion pace, not just final state.
hints.concept: Scheduling overhead and fairness choices both affect throughput.
hints.explicit: Run enough ticks to observe steady completion trend before submitting.

stages.objective: Diagnose throughput outcome with required completion checks.
stages.goal: Meet required completion while evaluating throughput quality.

hints.concept: Throughput claims are invalid if workload does not complete.
hints.explicit: Continue run until completion target is met, then verify other checks.

stages.objective: Apply tuning and preserve throughput/completion constraints.
stages.goal: Adjust policy within limits and keep required metrics satisfied.

hints.concept: Throughput improvements can regress fairness or response if over-tuned.
hints.explicit: Apply one policy change, rerun, and compare throughput plus completion.
